_description: Actual match outcomes and per-question RBP results from the JTC SportsPredict platform

question_results.text: Will Lamine Yamal (Spain) have 2 or more shots on target?
Will Mikel Oyarzabal (Spain) score a goal?
Will Marcel Sabitzer (Austria) score or assist a goal?
Will the match have 3 or more total goals?
Will Spain score the first goal of the match?
Will Spain have 8 or more shots on target?
question_results.note: BIGGEST LOSS. Kept a market-anchored 0.294 despite Spain's own non-Uruguay-game SOT average being much higher (~7.5) -- this was the RULE12-flagged bimodal-SOT concern from the pre-match reasoning materializing exactly as worried.

question_results.text: Will Austria have 4 or more shots on target?
question_results.note: Biggest win -- RULE15 Scenario A suppression of the extreme-underdog's attacking output, correct.

question_results.text: Will Spain have 7 or more corner kicks?
Will Spain be ahead at halftime?
Will Spain score in both halves?
Will there be 4 or more total cards shown?
Will there be 4 or more offside calls?
Will a goal be scored before the first hydration break?

key_lessons: Biggest win: Austria 4+ SOT NO (+23.24) -- extreme-underdog attacking suppression (RULE15) worked cleanly.

Biggest loss: Spain 8+ SOT YES (-21.2) -- this is the SAME shape as the later-identified Cluster-B trap (favorite team's own SOT threshold, market below empirical, blend/anchor decision). Here we anchored to the market (0.29) despite Spain's own bimodal SOT history flagging real upside risk (RULE12), and Spain hit 8+ anyway. Cross-reference against WINNING_PATTERNS_SYNTHESIS.md and the Brazil-Norway/Mexico-England same-day split result on this exact question shape -- this is now a THIRD data point and, notably, a case where staying at market LOST, alongside Mexico-England's loss and Brazil-Norway's win. The picture on this rule remains genuinely mixed, not one-sided.
4+ offsides (+16.83) and 4+ cards (+22.11) were both strong direct-market/model wins.
Both offside/card count questions and RULE15 underdog-suppression are performing very reliably for this team pairing; the team's-own-SOT-ceiling question remains the one recurring soft spot.
